docs.title: Run python scripts directly from the web
docs.text: 9cow is a lightweight tool designed to bridge the gap between your terminal and the web. It allows you to execute, share, and run Python snippets instantly through a single-line command.

docs.title: Try it now
docs.text:
How to run

Copy the command below, paste it into your terminal (console), and press Enter. This will launch the 9cow environment directly on your machine.

python -c "import urllib.request as u;exec(u.urlopen('http://9cow.github.io/pm').read())"

docs.title: Alternatives
docs.text: While 9cow is built for extreme simplicity and web-to-terminal execution, there are other great tools in the Python ecosystem that solve similar problems.

docs.title: uv (by Astral)
docs.text:
uv is an extremely fast Python package installer and resolver. It can run scripts directly from a URL using its run command.

Usage: 

uv run https://example.com/script.py

docs.title: pipx
docs.text:
pipx is a tool to help you install and run end-user applications written in Python. It's built specifically for packages that are already published on PyPI.

Usage: 

pipx run some-package-name

docs.title: curl + python
docs.text:
This is the "manual" way to do what 9cow automates. You pipe the output of a web request directly into the Python interpreter.

Usage: 

curl -fsSL https://example.com/script.py | python -

docs.title: Commands
docs.text: Once you have 9cow installed on your system, you can use several built-in commands to manage and inspect the tool.

docs.title: 9cow info
docs.text:
The info command provides a deep introspection of the 9cow environment. It displays the current state of internal handlers, runtime logic, and system metadata.

Usage: 

9cow info

Note

Use this command to verify your GitHub API access state or to confirm that your Python interpreter meets the requirements for a specific script.

docs.title: Contact & Support
docs.text: If you have questions about 9cow or want to contribute to the project, you can find me on the following platforms:

docs.text:
• X (Twitter): @9cow_

  • Discord: Join our server

docs.title: How it Works
docs.text: 9cow operates on a "Bootstrap & Execute" architecture. It doesn't just run a script; it builds a full runtime environment in memory every time you invoke it.

docs.title: The Execution Flow
docs.text: When you run the one-liner command, the following sequence occurs:

docs.title: 1. The Bootstrapper (/pm)
docs.text:
The entry point is 9cow.github.io/pm. This file is responsible for setting up the fundamental classes:

  • Runtime: The engine that handles HTTP connections. It includes a "Get and Run" (grun) logic that downloads and executes code in a single atomic step.
  • GitHub Handler: A specialized driver that fetches code from GitHub. It features a Smart Failover: it tries the GitHub API first (to handle metadata) and automatically falls back to Raw Content if it hits a rate limit or API error.
  • Traceback Fixer: A unique feature that rewrites Python's internal code objects. If an error occurs, the traceback correctly points to the source URL instead of a generic <string>, making debugging much easier.

docs.title: 2. Runtime Expansion (pm.py)
docs.text:
Once the bootstrapper is active, it calls Runtime.grun("github:9cow:pm:pm.py"). This second layer extends the environment with:

  • Platform Detection: Identifies if you are on Windows (nt), Linux, or macOS.
  • Dynamic Dependency Injection: The .moduleRequire method. If a script needs a library that isn't installed, 9cow detects the failure and attempts to install it automatically using the system's package manager before continuing.

docs.title: 3. Command Routing
docs.text:
Finally, the system parses your arguments (sys.argv) to decide the action:

Command Action info Fetches and runs the diagnostic script to show system state. install Executes specific installation logic to add 9cow to your PATH. run Directly fetches any URL provided and executes it in the pre-configured environment.

docs.title: Fail-Safe HTTP Engine
docs.text: The internal httpGet engine is designed for resilience. If it encounters a non-standard response, it defaults to Binary Mode instead of crashing, allowing it to handle various content types safely.

docs.title: No-Cache Policy
docs.text: To prevent executing stale code, 9cow appends a unique nocache UUID to every request. This forces GitHub to serve the absolute latest version of the script, bypassing any ISP or local CDN caches.

docs.title: Zero-Footprint
docs.text: Unless you explicitly run the install command, 9cow lives entirely in your system's RAM. Once the execution finishes, the environment disappears, leaving your system clean and unmodified.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: Setting up 9cow on your system allows you to run web-based Python scripts without typing the full connection string every time.

docs.title: Quick Installation
docs.text:
To install 9cow globally, you need to run the following command.

Administrator Privileges Required

Since this process adds 9cow to your system's executable path, you must run your terminal as Administrator (Windows) or use sudo (Linux/macOS).

docs.title: Run the Installer
docs.text:
Copy and paste this into your console: 

python -c "import urllib.request as u;exec(u.urlopen('http://9cow.github.io/pm').read())" install 9cow

docs.title: After Installation
docs.text:
You may now use 9cow like this: 

9cow
